comment: TFPT Nariai geometric quotient Q_geom=3/8 (the Koide-form quotient of the three horizon roots at the maximal de Sitter-black-hole/Nariai point; pure de Sitter limit = 1/2, horizon_readouts/v57) confronted with Jampolski & Rezzolla 2026 (arXiv:2509.15302) who find a MAXIMUM initial compactness C=3/8 above which collapse to a black hole is unavoidable (a de Sitter core nucleates and balances gravity -> gravastar). The shared rational 3/8 and shared de Sitter endpoint 1/2 motivate treating a C=3/8 object as a horizonless light-trapping ECO; combined with the TFPT recovery reflectivity it predicts an echo train. Compactness convention: C = G M /(R c^2) (geometric C=M/R), so the Schwarzschild horizon is C=1/2.

tfpt_prediction.echo_amplitude_ratio_label: (2/3)^6 = 64/729 (TFPT boundary-recovery reflectivity, same kernel as gw-ringdown-echo)
tfpt_prediction.note: Q_geom(Nariai)=3/8 vs Q_geom(de Sitter)=1/2; the ECO echo amplitude is bounded by the recovery eigenvalue (2/3)^6.

external_result.name: Jampolski & Rezzolla 2026, Formation of gravastars
external_result.max_compactness_label: C = 3/8 (above this, collapse to a black hole is unavoidable)

compactness_landmarks.photon_sphere_label: C=1/3 (R=3M): photon sphere at the surface -> light-trapping threshold
compactness_landmarks.buchdahl_label: C=4/9: Buchdahl bound for a static isotropic fluid

constants.comment: GM_sun/c^3 in seconds; geometric time unit per solar mass
